records.ety: [L. deliquescere to melt, dissolve; de- + liquescere to become fluid, melt, fr. liquere to be fluid. See Liquid.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deliquesced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deliquescing.]

defs: To dissolve gradually and become liquid by attracting and absorbing moisture from the air, as certain salts, acids, and alkalies.
quotes: In very moist air crystals of strontites deliquesce.
